verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein (CAP superfamily) with an N-terminal signal peptide but no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. It is expressed by epididymal epithelium, secreted into the epididymal lumen, and associates non-covalently with the sperm surface during spermatozoa maturation. The sperm-surface association (both loose and tight populations) is non-covalent and not a stable covalent linkage to a TM scaffold. Binding to the egg surface is similarly reversible. No cancer ecto-display, activation-induced surface translocation, or lysosomal exocytosis pathway is documented. Expression is restricted to epididymis/seminal plasma. The protein does not pass the wash test as a stably anchored surface molecule — it is recruited by sperm surface components, not anchored by its own mechanism.
